text: The System Administration Module shall operate on a Linux or Solaris server.
The System Administration Module shall be accessible through a web-browser or a Windows-compatible client.
The System Administration Module shall support Microsoft Internet Explorer (v.6.0 and later) and Mozilla Firefox (v.2.0 and later) if web-browser based.
The System Administration Module shall be accessible with screen-reading and screen-magnification software.
The System Administration Module shall use a fully relational database back-end.
The System Administration Module shall produce standards-compliant HTML.
The System Administration Module shall provide a development and training environment with the ability to migrate configurations to production.
The system shall provide an online hierarchical and cross-linked help system in HTML.
The system shall run on a fully relational SQL-based database system.
The system shall allow SQL queries against any table in the database.
The system shall provide database access as an ODBC source.
The system shall provide real-time processing for pull lists and shelf reading.
The system shall allow multiple staff members and patrons to simultaneously access and update patron and item records.
The system shall identify the location and user for any locked patron or item record.
The system shall support individual and shared staff login accounts with role-based access control.
The system shall provide access to all configuration files.
The system shall provide full access to all log files without stopping the system.
The system shall provide access to the root shell.
The system shall support the creation of custom dashboards for performance and transaction data.
The system shall support SNMP for monitoring system resources.
The system shall send configurable alerts via dashboards and email and text messages.
The system shall allow administrators to unlock records from a central console.
The system shall provide a performance dashboard identifying processes by ID and resource utilization.
The system shall provide a server management console with shutdown and restart utilities.
The system shall provide a management console for workstations running client software.
The system shall provide a circulation dashboard showing key performance indicators.
The system shall provide a single interface for reviewing and controlling scheduled tasks.
The system shall support SMTP for email transport.
The system shall support restrictions on deleting records based on business rules.
The system shall provide customizable rules for record suppression in staff and public interfaces.
The system shall allow the creation and modification of requesting rules for holds.
The system shall allow the creation and modification of loan rules for check-outs and renewals.
The system shall provide data validation and automatic formatting for any field.
The system shall perform live incremental and full backups of data and transaction logs.
The system shall log data changes and provide undo functionality.
The system shall support server clustering for failover capability.
The system shall support secure protocols including SFTP and SSL and SSH.
The system shall support batch import and export of MARC bibliographic and authority records.
The system shall support an unlimited number of record sets for batch updates and deletions.
The system shall provide dedicated interfaces for creating staff and patron accounts.
The system shall support administrator-programmable and user-programmable keyboard macros.
The system shall support central management of client software installation and updates.
The system shall store all client configuration files on the server.
The system shall allow customization of report layouts and output to CSV and Excel formats.
The system shall provide a query tool with Boolean operators and regular expression support.
The system shall provide fine-grained permissions for running specific or ad hoc reports.
The system shall support receipt and standard and label and forms printers.
The system shall provide the ability to print to a file or FTP or email or printer.
